reportSubtitle: CTA Engagement Analysis & Demo Funnel Review
summary:
Ads are performing well — both ad sets delivering leads at ~$90 CPL with consistent reach. The new enterprise landing page (live since Mar 21) is driving strong CTA engagement with 71 button clicks this week. See CTA click breakdown below.

The dual-tier funnel is working as designed — 'Start Free' got 12 clicks, showing real B2C self-serve interest — but we currently can't track whether those clicks become actual signups. Next steps: I'll request GTM access on the FinalBit app domain to close the attribution loop, clean up non-enterprise URL endpoints, and give targeting one more week of data before making larger changes.

metrics.label: Ad Spend
metrics.note: Split evenly between static and video ad sets.

metrics.label: Cost per Lead
metrics.note: Consistent across both ad sets. At this CPL, closing 1/10 enterprise leads is profitable.

metrics.label: Demos Booked
metrics.note: 1 no-show, 2 B2C, 1 B2B — need more data to know if this split is a pattern.

metrics.label: CTA Clicks (Enterprise Page)
metrics.note: Strong engagement across all pricing tiers on the new landing page.

funnel.title: Ad-to-Demo Funnel
funnel.notes: Big drop-off between demo CTA clicks (21) and actual bookings (4). Will continue tracking this.

findings.title: 'View Pricing' Dominates CTA Clicks at 46%
findings.description: Visitors want to see pricing before anything else. 'Book a Demo' follows at 28% combined, showing strong demo intent after price evaluation. The landing page structure is working.

findings.title: 19% Click-to-Booking Rate on Demo CTA
findings.description: 21 people clicked 'Book a Demo' but only 4 completed booking. 81% drop-off could indicate that customers clicking `Book a Demo` don't actually want to schedule one.

findings.title: 'Start Free' Gets 16% of Clicks — Dual-Tier Funnel Is Working
findings.description: 12 visitors clicked 'Start Free', validating the strategy of showing all pricing tiers. Whether these are converting to actual signups is unknown — we have no tracking on the app domain yet.

findings.title: 3 of 4 Demos Were B2C — But Sample Size Is Tiny
findings.description: Only 1 of 3 attended demos was a B2B enterprise prospect. At face value that's a 25% B2B qualification rate (~$362 effective enterprise CPL). 3 demos is too small to change targeting strategy.

actions.title: Analyzed PostHog CTA Data for Enterprise Page
actions.description: Cataloged all 71 CTA clicks by type and identified the 19% click-to-booking conversion gap.

actions.title: Request GTM Manager Access from FinalBit Team
actions.description: Will add conversion tracking on the app domain (finalbitai.com) to close the signup attribution loop.

actions.title: Remove/Reroute Non-Enterprise URL Endpoints
actions.description: Clean up homepage and /small-teams on the marketing site. All ad traffic should funnel through /enterprise only.

nextSteps: Monday Mar 30: Follow-up with Anna/Khachatur on demo quality and GTM access request.
Give targeting one more week before considering changes — landing page v2 is only 1 week old.
Once Google Tag Manager is on app domain, we'll track who actually converts by plan tier.
